# @zakkster/lite-headless/toast

Headless ephemeral notifications with auto-dismiss, swipe-to-dismiss,
pause-on-hover, stack management, and a live region for screen
readers.

## Quick example

```js
import { createToast } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/toast";

const toast = createToast({
    placement: "bottom-right",
    duration:  5000,
    maxStack:  5,
});

toast.attachRoot(viewportEl);

// Show a simple string toast
toast.show("Saved!");

// Show with custom element
const el = document.createElement("div");
el.className = "my-toast my-toast-success";
el.innerHTML = `
    <span class="icon">✓</span>
    <span class="message">Order placed</span>
    <button data-toast-close aria-label="Dismiss">×</button>
`;
toast.show(el, { duration: 3000 });

// Urgent (uses role="alert" -> assertive announcement)
toast.show("Connection lost", { urgent: true });
```

## Options

| Option            | Default          | Notes                                              |
| ----------------- | ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `placement`       | `"bottom-right"` | One of 6 standard placements (see below)           |
| `duration`        | `5000`           | ms before auto-dismiss; `0` = no auto-dismiss      |
| `swipeToDismiss`  | `true`           | pointer/touch swipe enabled                        |
| `swipeDirection`  | `"auto"`         | `"auto" | "left" | "right" | "up" | "down"`        |
| `swipeThreshold`  | `50`             | px of motion before commit-dismiss                 |
| `maxStack`        | `5`              | visible toast cap; overflow evicts oldest          |
| `pauseOnHover`    | `true`           | pointer over the stack freezes auto-dismiss        |
| `pauseOnFocus`    | `true`           | focus inside the stack freezes auto-dismiss        |
| `announceLive`    | `true`           | build a hidden aria-live region for status toasts  |
| `defaultUrgent`   | `false`          | role=alert vs role=status for toasts without opt   |
| `onShow`          | -                | `(id) => void`                                     |
| `onDismiss`       | -                | `(id, reason) => void`                             |

`reason` values: `"manual"`, `"auto-dismiss"`, `"swipe"`,
`"close-button"`, `"maxstack-evict"`, `"clear"`, `"destroy"`,
or any custom string passed to `dismiss(id, reason)`.

## Placement

Six standard placements determine WHERE in the viewport new toasts
appear and which direction the swipe-to-dismiss goes:

```
   top-left      top-center      top-right
   bottom-left   bottom-center   bottom-right
```

- **top-*** placements: new toasts insert at the TOP of the viewport
  (visual stack grows DOWNWARD away from the screen edge)
- **bottom-*** placements: new toasts append at the BOTTOM (visual
  stack grows UPWARD)

The placement also drives `swipeDirection: "auto"`:
- right placements → swipe right
- left placements → swipe left
- top-center → swipe up
- bottom-center → swipe down

The actual screen position of the viewport is **CSS-controlled** by
the consumer. The primitive only sets `data-placement="..."` on the
viewport; consumer CSS does the positioning:

```css
lite-toast {
    position: fixed;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.5rem;
    pointer-events: none;       /* let clicks through gaps */
}
lite-toast > * { pointer-events: auto; }

lite-toast[data-placement="bottom-right"] { bottom: 1rem; right: 1rem; }
lite-toast[data-placement="top-right"]    { top:    1rem; right: 1rem; }
/* ... */
```

## Attachment methods

| Method                | Returns      | Notes                                |
| --------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------------ |
| `attachRoot(el)`      | cleanup `()` | The viewport. role=region, hidden aria-live region appended |

## Imperative API

```js
toast.show(content, opts?)
// content: string | HTMLElement
// opts: { id?, duration?, urgent?, dismissible? }
// returns: { id, el, dismiss(reason?), update(content, opts?) }

toast.dismiss(id, reason?)
toast.clear(reason?)

toast.count()        // number of active toasts
toast.getEntries()   // [{ id, urgent }, ...]
toast.destroy()
```

The control object returned by `show()` is the easiest way to dismiss
or update a specific toast:

```js
const t = toast.show("Uploading...", { duration: 0, id: "upload" });
// ... later, when upload completes
t.update("Uploaded ✓", { duration: 2000 });
// ... or on error
t.dismiss("upload-failed");
```

## Close button auto-handling

Any descendant inside the toast element with `[data-toast-close]`
gets a click listener that dismisses the toast:

```html
<div class="my-toast">
    <span>Message</span>
    <button data-toast-close aria-label="Dismiss">×</button>
</div>
```

## Swipe-to-dismiss

The primitive listens for `pointerdown` on the toast and tracks the
gesture. During the swipe, it writes two CSS custom properties to
the toast element:

```css
--lh-toast-swipe-x: 42px;
--lh-toast-swipe-y: 0px;
```

Consumer CSS chooses what to do with the motion (translate, rotate,
opacity, etc.):

```css
[data-toast-id] {
    transform: translate(var(--lh-toast-swipe-x, 0), var(--lh-toast-swipe-y, 0));
}
[data-toast-id][data-swiping] {
    transition: none;          /* don't transition during the swipe */
    opacity: 0.7;
}
[data-toast-id][data-dismissing] {
    /* exit animation hook fires the moment commit happens */
    opacity: 0;
    transform: translateX(100%);
    transition: transform 200ms, opacity 200ms;
}
```

The primitive enforces the dismiss-direction: motion the wrong way
along the axis is clamped to zero (so a swipe RIGHT on a bottom-right
toast moves the toast right; swiping LEFT does nothing visible).
When the swipe distance exceeds `swipeThreshold`, the primitive
sets `data-dismissing="true"` and dismisses with `reason: "swipe"`.

Swipes that start on `<button>`, `<a>`, `<input>`, `<textarea>`,
or `<select>` children are ignored — so close buttons, links, and
inputs inside the toast remain interactive.

## Pause-on-hover / Pause-on-focus

When the user's pointer is over the stack (or focus is inside it),
auto-dismiss timers freeze. On hover-out / focus-out they resume
with the REMAINING time preserved — a toast that was 4 seconds into
a 5-second duration resumes with 1 second left, not a fresh 5.

The primitive sets `data-paused="true"` on each affected toast
during the pause (useful for visualising the freeze with a CSS
animation pause-state hook).

## ARIA painted attributes

| Element       | Attributes                                    |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Root          | `role="region"`, `aria-label="Notifications"`, `data-placement` |
| Toast (status)| `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`, `data-toast-id`, `data-placement` |
| Toast (urgent)| `role="alert"`, `aria-live="assertive"`, `aria-atomic="true"`   |
| Swiping toast | `data-swiping="true"`                         |
| Dismissing    | `data-dismissing="true"` (exit-animation hook)|
| Paused        | `data-paused="true"`                          |

A visually-hidden polite aria-live region is appended to the
viewport (unless `announceLive: false`). All non-urgent toasts
re-announce through it so screen readers consistently pick up the
content regardless of how the toast element is composed. Urgent
toasts skip the live region (their own `role="alert"` triggers a
direct assertive announcement).

## Custom element wrapper

```html
<lite-toast placement="bottom-right" duration="5000" max-stack="5"></lite-toast>

<script>
    const t = document.querySelector("lite-toast");
    document.getElementById("save-btn").addEventListener("click", async () => {
        const h = t.show("Saving...", { duration: 0, id: "save" });
        try {
            await api.save();
            h.update("Saved ✓", { duration: 2000 });
        } catch (e) {
            h.update("Failed: " + e.message, { duration: 4000, urgent: true });
        }
    });
</script>
```

Reactive attributes: none in v1.

Dispatched events:

- `show` — `{ detail: { id } }`
- `dismiss` — `{ detail: { id, reason } }`

## Comparison to other UI patterns

| Pattern        | Use toast for...                                            |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| Toast          | Ephemeral, non-blocking status updates                      |
| `<dialog>`     | Modal interactions that BLOCK until the user responds       |
| Inline message | Form validation, contextual info next to the source         |
| Snackbar       | Same primitive — "snackbar" is just bottom-center toast     |

Don't use toast for:
- Confirmations that need user action (use a dialog instead)
- Permanent notifications (use an inline message or notification panel)
- Errors the user MUST address before continuing (use a dialog)

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
